notes.note: Provincial commands were assigned to outgoing magistrates by lot (sortitio). Quintus Cicero, as propraetor, drew Asia, the wealthy province in western Anatolia, which he would govern 61--58 BC.
Philhellenes: the brothers' professed devotion to Greek culture, which Cicero invokes to urge Quintus toward a just and humane administration of the Greek-speaking province.
The chief Adriatic port of Italy (modern Brindisi), standard point of embarkation for travellers crossing to Greece and the East; Atticus had sailed from here toward Epirus.
The Ides fell on the 15th of March; here the dateline of the letter, 15 March 61 BC. (Famous for an event still twenty-seven years in the future.)
